personas.name: Aion Engine
personas.role: Using haematite as the EventStore backend

stories.text: As the Aion engine, I want to append events to a workflow key with optimistic concurrency so that two concurrent recorders to the same workflow are detected and one retries.
As the Aion engine, I want to batch-append WorkflowStarted and SearchAttributesUpdated atomically so that a crash between them never leaves an orphaned workflow.
As the Aion engine, I want to read a workflow's full event history in sequence order so that I can replay it for recovery.
As the Aion engine, I want to read events from a specific sequence number forward so that recovery resumes without re-reading the entire history.
As the Aion engine, I want to scan for workflows matching search attributes so that I can implement workflow visibility queries.
As the Aion engine, I want expired workflow histories to return a HistoryCompacted error so that I can distinguish compacted workflows from missing ones.

personas.name: Messaging Bus
personas.role: Using haematite for durable channel state

stories.text: As the messaging bus, I want to put and get conversation state by key so that durable channel state survives restarts.
As the messaging bus, I want to delete keys so that I can clean up closed conversations.
As the messaging bus, I want to set a TTL on consumer cursors so that abandoned cursors are automatically cleaned up without application-level sweep logic.
As the messaging bus, I want range queries to return keys in sorted order so that I can efficiently list conversations in a channel.

personas.name: Application Developer
personas.role: Embedding haematite as a general KV store

stories.text: As an application developer, I want a simple get/put/delete/range API so that I can use haematite without understanding prolly trees or WAL buffers.
As an application developer, I want commit to flush all pending writes and return root hashes so that I can control the durability boundary.
As an application developer, I want compare-and-swap on a single key so that I can implement optimistic concurrency for my own data structures.
As an application developer, I want to set TTL at write time so that I do not need to build my own expiry system.
As an operations engineer, I want to configure the sweep interval per-database so that I can balance cleanup frequency against scan cost.
As an operations engineer, I want the sweep actor to be independently supervised so that a sweep crash does not affect the shard actor's write availability.
